notes: Review #36 — code-only review (Puppeteer still blocked by container PID limits). ALL 12 issues from review #35 remain unfixed. No code changes detected since last review. Scores unchanged. Adding one new finding.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] Keyboard shortcuts '1' and '2' double-encode the hash
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 604-605: `window.location.hash = '#office'` and `'#dashboard'` — the '#' prefix causes the browser to produce `##office` / `##dashboard` in the URL. The hashchange listener slices only 1 '#', so browser back/forward navigation breaks. Fix: remove the '#' prefix. This was first reported in review #34.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] ProgressBar milestone Set never resets on task restart
suggestions.description: ProgressBar's `milestonesRef = useRef(new Set())` persists across task changes. When a worker finishes (100%) and starts a new task (0%), milestones 25/50/75/100 are already in the Set so flash animations never fire again. Fix: add `useEffect(() => { milestonesRef.current = new Set() }, [worker.task])` or key the component by task.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] Task-tag filter spans are mouse-only
suggestions.description: WorkerCard task tag `<span>` elements have onClick but no `role='button'`, `tabIndex={0}`, or `onKeyDown`. Keyboard users cannot focus or activate them. Screen readers don't know they're interactive.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x3] Character divs in Office lack role='button' and onKeyDown
suggestions.description: Office.jsx Character divs use `<div tabIndex={0}>` with onClick but no `role='button'` and no keyboard activation handler (Enter/Space). First reported review #33. This is a WCAG 2.1 AA failure.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] ProgressBar ETA uses updated_at instead of started_at
suggestions.description: App.jsx ProgressBar uses `worker.updated_at` (last data sync, ~30s ago) as task start time for ETA calculation, making it nonsensical. Either use `worker.started` (which exists in the data) or remove ETA entirely.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] activeWorkers computed without useMemo
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 637-639: `activeWorkers` is computed with `.filter()` directly in render body. useTimeTick(60_000) triggers re-renders that recompute this unnecessarily, defeating the downstream `useMemo(() => getTeamVibe(...))`. Wrap in useMemo.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] ActivityLog time-grouping runs 4 unfiltered passes per render
suggestions.description: ActivityLog `groups` maps over TIME_GROUP_ORDER calling `.filter()` for each label — O(4n) calls to getTimeGroup() every 60s tick. Wrap in useMemo([entries]).

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] WORKER_ROLE_COLORS keyed by name, not role
suggestions.description: App.jsx defines WORKER_ROLE_COLORS keyed by worker name (only 5 hardcoded). ROLE_COLORS in constants.js keys by role and handles any worker. Drop WORKER_ROLE_COLORS; use ROLE_COLORS[worker.role] in confetti logic.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] completionToast disappears instantly with no exit animation
suggestions.description: The completion toast has a nice entry animation but vanishes abruptly after 3000ms. Add a fade-out transition before nullifying state.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] Stuck worker stat card is keyboard-inaccessible
suggestions.description: StatsBar stuck worker card has onClick but no tabIndex, role, or onKeyDown. Also uses document.querySelector for scrolling — fragile in React. Add keyboard support and use refs.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] getRelativeTime duplicated between App.jsx and Office.jsx
suggestions.description: Identical time formatting logic exists in both files. Extract to src/utils/time.js.

suggestions.title: [UNFIXED x2] Office floor crushes characters below ~360px viewport
suggestions.description: No media query addresses office view at very narrow widths. Characters, nameplates, and speech bubbles overlap and clip.

suggestions.title: [NEW] ProgressBar uses updatedAt prop but should use worker.started for duration
suggestions.description: ProgressBar receives `updatedAt={worker.updated_at}` but the ETA calc needs task start time. The worker object has a `started` field that contains the actual task start timestamp. The ProgressBar component should receive `startedAt={worker.started}` instead. This is a data plumbing bug — the wrong field is being passed down.
